title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Why Do I Need a Child Plan?
In today's world, the cost of education and living are steadily increasing, every parent wants to ensure their child has the best opportunities in life. Securing your child’s financial future has never been more important. This is why a child's life insurance plan is more important. It combines financial security and disciplined savings, ensuring your child’s milestones such as education, marriage, or entrepreneurial dreams are not hindered. If questions like "Why should I consider a child plan?" or "Can a child life insurance plan secure my child’s future?" cross your mind, this article will explain why a child's life insurance plan is really important[1].
What Is a Child Plan?
A child life insurance plan is a financial plan that goes hand in hand with insurance and investment. Designed to secure significant life milestones such as higher education, marriage, or other pursuits, it offers financial protection against unforeseen circumstances[4].
Key Features of a Child Plan
1. Securing Education Costs
The cost of higher education, especially for professional courses like engineering and medicine, has been rising, with studying abroad being even more expensive. A child plan helps parents save systematically over time, ensuring they are financially ready for these future education costs.
2. Tax Advantages
Tax benefits under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act are offered under Child plans (in case of old tax regime), allowing parents to save on taxable income. Additionally, the maturity benefits are often exempt from tax under Section 10(10D), subject to prevailing tax laws. [1]3. Flexibility in Pay-outs
Most child plans provide flexibility to structure pay-outs. These pay-outs can be scheduled to coincide with milestones such as college admissions or marriage. For instance, periodic pay-outs can help cover tuition fees efficiently.
4. Encouraging Financial Discipline
A child plan encourages parents to invest systematically for the long term. This ensures funds are available when required, fostering a disciplined approach toward financial planning.
Why Invest in a Child Plan?
1. Building Wealth Early
Investing early enables parents to benefit from compounding returns. Starting a plan when the child is young helps lock in lower premiums and accumulate a substantial corpus over time.
2. Protecting Future Aspirations
Every child’s dreams, whether pursuing higher studies abroad, starting a business, or exploring a passion, require financial support. A child plan ensures these aspirations are safeguarded against financial constraints.
3. Safeguarding Against Life’s Uncertainties
Life is unpredictable, and uncertainties like medical emergencies or the loss of a parent can disrupt financial plans. A child plan offers a robust safety net, ensuring the child’s needs are met regardless of life’s challenges.
4. Comprehensive Coverage
Many child plans offer additional benefits like critical illness coverage and premium waiver riders, enhancing their utility and providing extra security for the child’s future.[5]
Features of a Good Child Plan
When choosing a child plan, it is crucial to align it with your financial goals and your child’s future needs. Key features to consider include:
1. Premium Waiver Benefit
In case of the policyholder’s demise, this feature ensures that future premiums are waived, and the policy benefits continue as planned. [2]
2. Partial Withdrawals
Some child plans allow partial withdrawals at specific intervals, making it easier to fund urgent needs like tuition fees or extracurricular activities.
3. Maturity Benefits
Child plans typically offer maturity benefits, ensuring a significant sum is available when required for major life milestones.
4. Customizable Coverage
Many insurers offer customizable plans, enabling parents to tailor coverage to meet specific needs like higher education or marriage.
Important Considerations When Choosing a Child Plan
1. Understand the Costs
Anticipate the costs of your child’s future needs, such as higher education or starting a business. Use these estimates to determine the required investment.
2. Compare Options
Research and compare plans from reputed insurers. Focus on features like flexible payouts, competitive returns, and additional riders. Ensure the plan meets your specific requirements.
3. Review Terms and Conditions
Carefully review the policy’s inclusions, exclusions, and charges to avoid surprises later.
For example, Raj invests ₹50,000 annually in a child plan for 15 years. When Aarav turns 18, the plan matures, providing ₹12 lakh to fund his higher education, ensuring Raj’s financial planning supports his child’s goals.
Conclusion
Investing in a child's life insurance plan is a thoughtful and proactive step toward securing your child’s future. By offering financial protection, disciplined savings, and tax benefits, these plans ensure that your child’s aspirations remain unaffected by financial challenges. Start early, research thoroughly, and select a plan that aligns with your financial goals to provide your child with a strong foundation for their dreams.
FAQs
What is the ideal time to invest in a child plan?The best time to invest in a child plan is when your child is young. Early investment maximizes compounding benefits and lowers premium costs.
What are the tax benefits of a child plan?Premiums paid are eligible for tax benefits under Section 80C (under old tax regime), while maturity proceeds are tax-exempt under Section 10(10D), subject to prevailing laws. [1]
How does a child plan help with education expenses?A child plan helps parents systematically save and accumulate funds over time, ensuring they are financially prepared to meet the rising costs of their child’s education, especially higher studies.
[1]https://www.etmoney.com/learn/personal-finance/everything-you-need-to-know-about-child-education-plans/ [2]https://www.investopedia.com/terms/w/waiver_of_premium.asp [3] https://www.investopedia.com/terms/u/unit-linked-insurance-plan.asp [4]https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/industry/banking/finance/insure/insurance-for-all-irdai-urges-cos-to-strategize-making-insurance-affordable/articleshow/112800150.cms?from=mdr [5]https://www.indmoney.com/blog/insurance/best-child-insurance-plans-in-indiaDisclaimer
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. full_pdf_text:
Why Do I Need a Child Plan?
In today's world, the cost of education and living are steadily increasing, every parent wants to ensure their child has the best opportunities in life. Securing your child’s financial future has never been more important. This is why a child's life insurance plan is more important. It combines financial security and disciplined savings, ensuring your child’s milestones such as education, marriage, or entrepreneurial dreams are not hindered. If questions like "Why should I consider a child plan?" or "Can a child life insurance plan secure my child’s future?" cross your mind, this article will explain why a child's life insurance plan is really important[1].
What Is a Child Plan?
A child life insurance plan is a financial plan that goes hand in hand with insurance and market-linked investment. Designed to secure significant life milestones such as higher education, marriage, or other pursuits, it offers financial protection against unforeseen circumstances[4].
Key Features of a Child Plan
1. Securing Education Costs
The cost of higher education, especially for professional courses like engineering and medicine, has been rising, with studying abroad being even more expensive. A child plan helps parents save systematically over time, ensuring they are financially ready for these future education costs.
2. Tax Advantages
Tax benefits under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act are offered under Child plans (in case of old tax regime), allowing parents to save on taxable income. Additionally, the maturity benefits are often exempt from tax under Section 10(10D), subject to prevailing tax laws. [1]
3. Flexibility in Pay-outs
Most child plans provide flexibility to structure pay-outs. These pay-outs can be scheduled to coincide with milestones such as college admissions or marriage. For instance, periodic pay-outs can help cover tuition fees efficiently.
4. Encouraging Financial Discipline
A child plan encourages parents to invest systematically for the long term. This ensures funds are available when required, fostering a disciplined approach toward financial planning.
Why Invest in a Child Plan?
1. Building Wealth Early
Investing early enables parents to benefit from compounding returns. Starting a plan when the child is young helps lock in lower premiums and accumulate a substantial corpus over time.
2. Protecting Future Aspirations
Every child’s dreams, whether pursuing higher studies abroad, starting a business, or exploring a passion, require financial support. A child plan ensures these aspirations are safeguarded against financial constraints.
3. Safeguarding Against Life’s Uncertainties
Life is unpredictable, and uncertainties like medical emergencies or the loss of a parent can disrupt financial plans. A child plan offers a robust safety net, ensuring the child’s needs are met regardless of life’s challenges.
4. Comprehensive Coverage
Many child plans offer additional benefits like critical illness coverage and premium waiver riders, enhancing their utility and providing extra security for the child’s future.[5]
Features of a Child Plan
When choosing a child plan, it is crucial to align it with your financial goals and your child’s future needs. Key features to consider include:
1. Premium Waiver Benefit
In case of the policyholder’s demise, this feature ensures that future premiums are waived, and the policy benefits continue as planned. [2]
2. Partial Withdrawals
Unit Linked Insurance Plan (ULIP) based child plans allow partial withdrawals at specific intervals, subject to policy terms & conditions, making it easier to fund urgent needs like tuition fees or extracurricular activities.
3. Maturity Benefits
Child plans typically offer maturity benefits, ensuring a significant sum is available when required for major life milestones.
4. Customizable Coverage
Many insurers offer customizable plans, enabling parents to tailor coverage to meet specific needs like higher education or marriage.
Important Considerations When Choosing a Child Plan
1. Understand the Costs
Anticipate the costs of your child’s future needs, such as higher education or starting a business. Use these estimates to determine the required investment.
2. Compare Options
Research and compare plans from reputed insurers. Focus on features like flexible payouts, competitive returns, and additional riders. Ensure the plan meets your specific requirements.
3. Review Terms and Conditions
Carefully review the policy’s inclusions, exclusions, and charges to avoid surprises later.
Conclusion
Opting for a child's life insurance plan is a thoughtful and proactive step toward securing your child’s future. By offering financial protection, disciplined savings, and tax benefits, these plans ensure that your child’s aspirations remain unaffected by financial challenges. Start early, research thoroughly, and select a plan that aligns with your financial goals to provide your child with a strong foundation for their dreams.
FAQs
1. What is the ideal time to opt for a child plan?
The best time to purchase a child plan is when your child is young. Early investment maximizes compounding benefits and lowers premium costs. 2. What are the tax benefits of a child plan?
Premiums paid are eligible for tax benefits under Section 80C (under old tax regime), while maturity proceeds are tax-exempt under Section 10(10D), subject to prevailing laws. [1]
3. How does a child plan help with education expenses?
A child plan helps parents systematically save and accumulate funds over time, ensuring they
are financially prepared to meet the rising costs of their child’s education, especially higher studies.

The policyholder will not be able to surrender or withdraw the monies invested in Unit Linked Insurance Products completely or partially till the end of the fifth year. ULIPs are different from the traditional insurance products and are subject to the risk factors. The premium paid in ULIPs are subject to investment risks associated with capital markets and the NAVs of the units may go up or down based on the performance of fund and factors influencing the capital market and the insured is responsible for his/her decisions. Please know the associated risks and the applicable charges, from your Insurance agent or the Intermediary or policy document issued by the insurance company. The various funds offered under this contract are the names of the funds and do not in any way indicate the quality of these plans, their future prospects and returns. The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions.
